seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Pydantic AI enables type-safe, production-ready conversational AI pipelines that process real-time speech with structured LLM outputs. Guardrails are essential due to the high-stakes nature of voice interactions, where latency, safety, and coherence failures can damage user trust and brand reputation. Vex provides runtime verification to catch subtle issues like validator rejections and streaming failures before they impact live conversations.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents follow a real-time pipeline: microphone audio streams to a speech-to-text service (Whisper/FasterWhisper), generating text transcripts fed into a Pydantic AI agent for reasoning and tool execution. The agent produces structured JSON responses validated against Pydantic models, which are then converted to speech via text-to-speech (ElevenLabs/PlayHT) for sub-second round-trip latency. 

Pydantic AI's Agent class handles conversation state and tool calls, with result validators ensuring output schemas match expected formats. Nested tool calls for database lookups or API integrations must maintain dependency context, while streaming responses enable low-latency partial TTS generation. The pipeline requires end-to-end monitoring for latency budgets under 500ms.

Critical components include WebSocket connections for bidirectional audio/text streaming, async event loops for non-blocking processing, and safety filters to block harmful responses before TTS synthesis.

risks.name: Validator rejecting valid but unexpected outputs
risks.description: LLM generates semantically correct responses that fail Pydantic schema validation due to minor formatting differences
risks.mitigation: Vex result validators with fuzzy matching and retry logic before schema enforcement

risks.name: Tool retry masking persistent errors
risks.description: Agent retries failed tool calls indefinitely without surfacing root cause database/API issues
risks.mitigation: Vex max_retry limits and error classification to trigger human intervention

risks.name: Streaming validation failures mid-generation
risks.description: Partial streaming responses fail validation, causing entire conversation turn to drop
risks.mitigation: Vex incremental streaming validators with graceful degradation

risks.name: Missing safety filtering in real-time responses
risks.description: Unfiltered LLM outputs bypass content moderation before TTS synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex safety classifiers running parallel to main pipeline

implementation.title: Basic Voice Agent with Pydantic AI
implementation.description: Core agent setup with speech-to-text integration and structured response validation
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
import asyncio
from openai import AsyncOpenAI

class VoiceResponse(BaseModel):
    response_text: str = Field(..., max_length=200)
    intent: str
    confidence: float

@tool
async def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    return f"Weather in {city}: 72°F sunny"

voice_agent = Agent(
    'gpt-4o-mini',
    result_model=VoiceResponse,
    tools=[get_weather],
    model_kwargs={'temperature': 0.1}
)

async def process_voice_input(audio_text: str) -> VoiceResponse:
    return await voice_agent.run(audio_text)

# Usage
result = asyncio.run(process_voice_input('What's the weather in SF?'))
print(result.response_text)

implementation.title: Real-time Streaming Pipeline
implementation.description: WebSocket handler for continuous speech processing with sub-second latency
implementation.code:
import websockets
import json
from faster_whisper import WhisperModel

whisper_model = WhisperModel("base", device="cpu")

async def voice_websocket_handler(websocket):
    async for message in websocket:
        # Speech-to-text
        segments, _ = whisper_model.transcribe(message, beam_size=5)
        transcript = ' '.join(seg.text for seg in segments)
        
        # Pydantic AI processing
        result = await process_voice_input(transcript)
        
        # Send TTS payload
        await websocket.send(json.dumps({
            'tts_text': result.response_text,
            'confidence': result.confidence
        }))

# Run server
start_server = websockets.serve(voice_websocket_handler, 'localhost', 8765)
asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(start_server)
asyncio.get_event_loop().run_forever()

implementation.title: Conversation State Management
implementation.description: Maintaining multi-turn context with dependency injection for tools
implementation.code:
from typing import Dict, Any

class ConversationState:
    def __init__(self):
        self.history: list = []
        self.user_context: Dict[str, Any] = {}

async def process_with_state(state: ConversationState, input_text: str) -> VoiceResponse:
    context = {
        'history': state.history[-5:],
        'user_id': state.user_context.get('user_id')
    }
    
    result = await voice_agent.run(input_text, context=context)
    state.history.append({'input': input_text, 'output': result})
    return result

# Stateful processing
state = ConversationState()
result1 = await process_with_state(state, 'Hi, I need restaurant recs')
result2 = await process_with_state(state, 'Italian please')

implementation.title: Latency Monitoring Wrapper
implementation.description: Pipeline wrapper measuring end-to-end latency budgets
implementation.code:
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def latency_monitor(stage: str, budget_ms: int):
    start = time.time()
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        duration = (time.time() - start) * 1000
        if duration > budget_ms:
            print(f'⚠️ {stage} exceeded budget: {duration:.0f}ms > {budget_ms}ms')

async def monitored_pipeline(audio: bytes) -> str:
    with latency_monitor('stt', 150):
        transcript = await stt_pipeline(audio)
    
    with latency_monitor('llm', 250):
        response = await process_voice_input(transcript)
    
    with latency_monitor('tts', 100):
        audio_out = await tts_synthesize(response.response_text)
    
    return audio_out

vexIntegration.title: Vex Result Validator on Agent
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as primary result validator to catch schema failures early
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex, Guardrail
from pydantic_ai import Agent

vex = Vex(api_key='vex-your-key')

@vex.guardrail('voice-agent-safety')
def safety_filter(content: str) -> bool:
    return 'harmful' not in content.lower()

voice_agent_with_vex = Agent(
    'gpt-4o-mini',
    result_model=VoiceResponse,
    result_validator=[
        Guardrail('pydantic-validation'),
        Guardrail('voice-latency', max_duration_ms=400),
        safety_filter
    ],
    tools=[get_weather]
)

# Vex monitors all runs automatically
result = await voice_agent_with_vex.run('user input')

# Check guardrail verdicts
verdicts = vex.get_latest_verdicts('voice-agent-safety')
if not all(v.passed for v in verdicts):
    print('❌ Guardrail failure detected')

vexIntegration.title: Streaming Response Validation
vexIntegration.description: Incremental validation for streaming TTS generation
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import StreamingGuardrail

@vex.streaming_guardrail('voice-streaming')
def validate_streaming_chunks(chunks: list[str]) -> bool:
    partial = ''.join(chunks)
    return (
        len(partial) < 200 and  # Length budget
        safety_filter(partial) and  # Safety
        partial.endswith(('.', '!', '?'))  # Coherent sentence
    )

async def stream_voice_response(input_text: str):
    stream = voice_agent_with_vex.stream(input_text)
    
    async for chunk in stream:
        if not validate_streaming_chunks(chunk):
            # Fallback to safe response
            yield 'I need to think about that...'
            return
        yield chunk

# Usage in WebSocket
async for tts_chunk in stream_voice_response(transcript):
    await websocket.send(tts_chunk)
